# task\-graphify\-brief\-enrichment\-correctness — Make Graphify brief enrichment compatible, relevant, and deterministic
status: in_progress  priority: 1  scope: scope-core
readiness: not_eligible  reason: status_in_progress

Fix Graphify\-based brief enrichment so current Graphify output is accepted and task\-specific results are safe, relevant, deterministic, and efficient\. Empty source paths currently match every hint; insertion order controls truncation; symbol nodes duplicate file\-level output; Graphify 0\.9\.23 links/null/community fields are rejected silently; impact wording ignores undirected graphs; and the configured defaults\.brief\_budget is not honored when callers omit a budget\.

## Acceptance
- Accept Graphify graphs with either edges or links, nullable optional fields, missing concepts, community metadata, and directed metadata\.
- Malformed or schema\-incompatible graph data returns an explicit warning diagnostic while missing graph data still degrades cleanly\.
- Blank source paths never match hints or appear in related files or impact hints\.
- Match normalized hints with path\-boundary semantics, deduplicate by canonical file, rank exact and directory matches before connected files, and truncate only after deterministic ranking\.
- Graph traversal uses node and adjacency indexes rather than repeated full\-array scans\.
- Impact output deduplicates files, excludes self\-file edges, and uses neutral language for undirected graphs\.
- Concepts can be derived from current Graphify node community metadata when no top\-level concepts array exists\.
- buildBrief honors defaults\.brief\_budget when no explicit budget is supplied, while explicit caller budgets continue to override it\.
- Regression tests cover empty paths, duplicate symbols, shuffled arrays, links/null compatibility, post\-ranking truncation, overlapping hints, directed and undirected wording, diagnostics, and configured budgets\.
- All tests, typecheck, Biome check, graphify update, compiled binary build, and ledger validation pass\.
